Using format provided for the Google slides updates


Summary of May 7th group meeting.
1) presented some test benches and the output graphs from the simulations.
2) discussed the issues I was having, problems with cadence, etc. only graph of any relevance remaining importance is attached bellow. Graph is of 3 synapses receiving 3 different spike trains generated by a python script implementing a Poisson distribution. 
3) key takeaways for me from the meeting. 
	1) explanation of what a final chart/graph should be for displaying linear/non linear region of the synapse dynamics
	2) suggestion of how to fix the issues that I was having with cadence, just use a resistor to prevent short and an error on my part in how I connected the circuit.
	3) spike width should be around 10u
**![528](data:image/png;base64, "dtau3syn1-2outputgraph.png")**

Planning; to do list: 
[[1_to-do]]

Tasks completed: 
1) all of the following was made with data from a test simulation that. simulation test data, spans from 1hz to 561hz in 80hz increments for 1 trial and implements  +aps and mt=2 to speed up the simulation at the expense of accuracy and number of data points. despite this simulations took 2-3h
2) wrote scripts for generating n spike trains with Poisson distribution at different frequencies. python script can be found in /sebastian_thesis_repo/processing/general_code/sdgo_stepsize.py
3) wrote scripts for simulating 2 input spike trains into 1 synapse, and another script for 2 synapses with 1 input spike train to run Cadence spectre simulation, and some optimizations made.  single_pwl_opt.ocn and duo_pwl_opt.ocn respectively. they are both in the directory /sebastian_thesis_repo/processing/general_code/
4) wrote script to convert output txt files to csv files
5) wrote a script to graph the output spike trains and input spike trains images can be found in /. under the name of simulation_results0... such as [[simulation_results0_4.png]]
6) wrote a script to plot the area difference between the 1syn and 2syn current outputs for different frequencies. [[simulation_results_height_map.png]]
7) read some more of the research papers, and took some notes

Key Results and Discussion:
1) Noticed that when there are overlapping input spikes into 1synapse, nonlinear behavior isn't really observed, it simply acts as though it were a wider input spike. I anticipate that this is due to the fact that the input spikes were 1.8V, and hence when there was any increase in voltage from another input spike the input nmos was already in the supra threshold regime and thus there was no effect. my proposal for investigation, for what to simulate next would be reducing the input spike voltage to bellow 1.8 volts, and perhaps have the spikes randomly assigned a voltage between 1.8V and 0V following some distribution i.e. a normal distribution.
2) Simulations in cadence take a really long time. I could reduce the the time as simulations were simulating 1s, however then I would need to increase the number of trials to compensate. a obvious solution to test would be to increase simulation multithreading m=4 or higher and use ++aps, even if there is reduced accuracy.  Biggest improvement to simulation time would be to use multiple jobs. I just need to figure out how to make this "compatible" with my approach of using ocean scripts for exporting specter simulation data.
3) Some error introduced from the approach of making the pandas data frames the same shape for analysis. when csv's did not have the same shape but I needed to subtract the difference of columns from two different csv's. "generated" "new data" by creating new points and taking the average of the previous and next point where necessary. this causes some error as seen in the last graph in [[simulation_results0_5_81_401hz_spike_after_simultaneous_spike.png]] (incorrectly named following my first assumption by looking at the graphs and seeing that this seemed more prevalent after the first simultaneous spike). this effect had a non-zero, but seemingly negligible effect on [[simulation_results_height_map.png]] 

Priority work next week.
1) get simulation to work with multiple jobs so that I can run it faster and not have expected wait times of 70h+.
2) figure out how to get the simulations, running without interruption unattended.
3) optimize parameters of simulation, so that I am not wasting valuable compute on unnecessary simulations. 
4) hopefully while the simulations are running, finish reading research papers, generalize the scripts to function for n synapses and set up a more general test bench that would allow me to run one continuous simulation that sweeps 2 to 64 synapses. 
5) adjust input spike voltage to hopefully see more non linear behavior. 
6) rest of to do list. [[1_to-do]]



Addition of completed work on 18th and 19th of May:
- got parallelization working, (required ipcBeginProcess and modification to ocean script.)
